content: Prince Harry left the charity last year in an acrimonious dispute over how Sentebale was being managed.

originalTitle: Chimpanzees in Uganda locked in vicious 'civil war', say researchers
title: Chimps in Uganda are Having a Big Disagreement!
content:
Hello Amazing Kids from Africa!

Hello wonderful children! It’s your friend here, reporting live from our amazing continent, Africa! I have an important story to share with you today. It’s about some very special animals who live here, just like us. We are going to talk about chimpanzees! They are super smart and sometimes act a lot like people. Let's learn about them and a bit of a sad story happening in their world.

Meet Our Chimp Friends!

Chimpanzees are one of Africa's most famous animals. They are big, strong monkeys, but they are actually called 'apes'. Apes are a type of monkey without a tail! Chimps have dark fur and very clever eyes. They live in forests and love to swing through trees. They eat fruits, leaves, and sometimes even small insects. Chimps are very social, which means they like to live together in big groups. They play, groom each other, and even share food. It's like a big family! Did you know? Chimpanzees can use tools! They can use sticks to get yummy ants out of holes or stones to crack nuts. How clever is that?

Uganda: A Beautiful Home

Our story takes us to a beautiful country in Africa called Uganda. Uganda is a place with lots of green forests, tall mountains, and big lakes. Many different animals live there, including elephants, lions, and, of course, chimpanzees! The weather in Uganda is often warm and sunny, which is perfect for these amazing animals. The forests provide everything the chimpanzees need to live happy lives. Did you know? Uganda is sometimes called the 'Pearl of Africa' because it's so beautiful and green!

The Ngogo Chimps: A Big Family

In one special forest in Uganda, there lives a very big group of chimpanzees. They are called the Ngogo chimpanzees. For a very long time, these Ngogo chimps were like one big, happy family. They lived together, played together, and helped each other. They were a 'close-knit community', which means they were all very good friends and looked out for one another. Imagine your best friends, your family, and all your classmates being one super team – that's how they used to be. They had leaders, just like in your school or your home. Everyone knew their place, and they mostly lived in peace. They shared their food and their home in the forest. It was a wonderful place to be a chimp.

A Sad Story: Friends Fighting

But something sad started to happen about eight years ago. Eight years is a very long time! It's like the difference between being a baby and being in Class 2 now. For eight whole years, the Ngogo chimps have been having a big, big disagreement. The grown-ups who study them, called 'researchers', say they are 'at loggerheads'. This means they are having a really serious fight, almost like a 'civil war' for them. It’s like when friends or family members stop being kind to each other and start arguing a lot. These chimps, who used to be one big happy family, have now split into two different groups. They are not friends anymore. It's a very sad thing to see friends fighting for such a long time.

Why Are They Fighting?

You might wonder, why would chimpanzees fight so much? Well, it's a bit like why people sometimes argue. They might be fighting over things like food. If there isn't enough yummy fruit for everyone, some chimps might get greedy. Or maybe they are fighting over their 'territory'. A territory is like their home patch in the forest. Each group wants a big, safe piece of the forest for themselves. They might also be fighting over who gets to be the boss. In chimpanzee groups, there's always a leader, called the 'alpha male'. Sometimes, other chimps want to be the leader too, and that can cause big fights. These fights can be very serious, and sometimes chimps can get hurt. It's a big problem for their once peaceful lives. Did you know? Chimps communicate with each other using many different sounds, like hoots, barks, and screams!

What Do Grown-Ups Do?

The people who study these chimps are called 'researchers' or 'scientists'. They watch the chimpanzees every day to understand what is happening. They write down notes and take pictures. They try to understand why the chimps are fighting and what it means for their community. It's like a detective trying to solve a mystery! They want to learn how animals live and what makes them happy or sad. They hope to understand animal behavior better. These researchers are very careful not to disturb the chimps. They just watch from a distance. Their job is to tell the world about what they see. They help us learn more about our animal friends and how we can protect them. Did you know? Researchers sometimes give the chimpanzees special names to tell them apart, just like you have a name!

What Can We Learn?

This story about the Ngogo chimpanzees is a little sad, but it can teach us something important. It shows us that even in nature, friends can have big disagreements. But it also reminds us how important it is to try and be kind to each other. When we work together, play together, and share, everyone is happier. Fighting can make everyone sad and cause problems. It reminds us to try and solve our problems with words, not with arguments or anger. Being a good friend means listening and trying to understand. If we learn to be kind and share, our communities will be strong and happy, just like the Ngogo chimps used to be.

Let's Be Kind!

So, my dear friends, remember the Ngogo chimpanzees. Let's hope they can find a way to be friends again. And let's remember to be kind to our own friends, our family, and everyone around us. Sharing, talking, and being friendly makes our world a much better place. From Africa, with love and kindness, that's your news for today! Keep smiling!
summary: A large group of chimpanzees in Uganda called the Ngogo chimps, who used to be like one big happy family, have been having a big disagreement and fighting for eight years, making researchers wonder why their community broke apart.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us about animals, like how smart chimpanzees are, and it reminds us how important it is for friends and families to be kind, share, and solve problems without fighting so everyone can live happily together.

originalTitle: Popular Tanzanian musician Matonya charged with rape in Kenya
title: A Musician in Court: Matonya's Story Explained for Kids!
content:
Hello, wonderful friends! Welcome to our special news report from our amazing continent, Africa. Today, we have a story about a musician that we want to tell you about in a way that's easy to understand. It's about a man named Matonya. He is a famous singer from a country called Tanzania, which is a beautiful place in East Africa known for Mount Kilimanjaro!

What Happened with Matonya?

Matonya is 43 years old, which is older than your teacher but maybe younger than your grandparents! He makes lovely music that many people enjoy listening to. But something important happened recently. Matonya was 'charged' with a very serious problem in another African country called Kenya. When someone is 'charged,' it means that someone else has said they did something very, very wrong. It's like when your mom or dad asks you, 'Did you do this?' and they think you might have done something naughty. But remember, being charged doesn't mean it's true yet! This is a very important part of our news. Matonya said 'No, I didn't do it!' He 'denies' the charge. To deny something means to say that it is not true. He believes he is innocent, which means he says he did nothing wrong. It's important for everyone to know that when someone is accused of something, they get to say if it's true or not. Did you know? Tanzania is home to the Serengeti National Park, where you can see amazing animals like lions, giraffes, and elephants!

Going to Court to Find the Truth

When there is a big problem like this, and someone says 'Yes, you did!' and the other person says 'No, I didn't!', we need a special place to help figure out the truth. This special place is called a 'court'. In court, there are special people who help. There are police officers who help find out what happened. There are lawyers who help people tell their story. And there is a very important person called a 'judge'. The judge is like a grown-up who listens to everyone very carefully. The judge makes sure that everyone gets a fair chance to speak. It's like when you and your friend have a disagreement, and an adult helps you both explain your side so things can be fair. That's what courts do for bigger problems. Did you know? Kenya is famous for its amazing wildlife safaris and the Great Rift Valley, a place with stunning landscapes and lakes!

What is 'Bail' and a 'Passport'?

After Matonya was charged, the court made a decision. They said he could go home for now, but under a special rule. This rule is called 'bail'. 'Bail' is like a promise. The judge lets someone go home from court, but only if they promise to come back to court whenever they are called. Sometimes, people have to give some money to the court as part of their promise, and they get it back if they come back. So, Matonya was 'released on bail'. This means he is not in a jail cell right now. He is at home, but he has promised to come back to court to continue telling his story and listening to what the judge says. But there was another important thing Matonya had to do. He had to give his 'passport' to the court. Do you know what a passport is? A passport is a very important little book that grown-ups use when they want to travel to different countries. It shows who you are and where you come from. When Matonya gave his passport to the court, it means he cannot travel outside of Kenya right now. This makes sure he stays in Kenya and comes back to court when he needs to. Did you know? Every country in the world has its own laws to help people live together safely and fairly. It's like having rules at school or at home!

Why is This Important?

This story about Matonya helps us understand how justice works in many countries, including our amazing countries in Africa. It shows that when there is a big problem or a serious accusation, there is a special process to follow. Everyone, no matter how famous or important they are, must follow the rules and go to court if they are accused of something serious. And everyone has the right to say they are innocent and to have a judge listen carefully to their side of the story. It's all about making sure things are fair and that the truth is found. It can take a little while for the court to figure everything out, but it's important to be patient and let the process work. The court will listen to all the information and then make a decision. Did you know? There are 54 countries in Africa, and each one has its own special culture, languages, and stories! So, that's the story of Matonya. He is a musician from Tanzania who was charged with a serious problem in Kenya. He says he is innocent, and now the court will help to find out the truth. We will keep you updated as we learn more! Stay curious and keep learning about our wonderful world!

originalTitle: Video: Vance arrives in Pakistan for talks with Iran
title: Good News! Important Man Flies Far to Talk About Peace!
content:
Jambo, Superstars of Africa!

Hello, hello, hello, my wonderful friends! It's your friendly reporter, Kito, here from our amazing continent of Africa! I have some really big news to share with you today, and it's all about making the world a happier, more peaceful place. Are you ready? Today, we're talking about an important man who flew a very long way. He travelled to a country called Pakistan. And guess what? He went there to talk! Like when you talk to your friends to make things better.

Who is This Important Man?

His name is Mr. Vance. He is a very, very important person from a country called America. In America, they have a big leader called the President. Mr. Vance is like the President's super helper! He helps the President with all the important jobs. Did you know? A Vice President helps the main leader, just like your head prefect helps the head teacher in school! They work together to make sure everything runs smoothly. Mr. Vance has a very big job, and part of his job is to help solve problems between countries. He wants to help everyone live together happily.

Where Did He Go?

Mr. Vance got on a big airplane and flew very, very far away! He landed in a country called Pakistan. Imagine flying for many, many hours, high up in the sky, over lands and oceans! That's how far he went. Pakistan is a beautiful country. It has lots of people, big mountains, and sometimes even hot deserts! It is in a part of the world called Asia. It's not in Africa, but it's a very important place right now. Did you know? Pakistan is home to some of the world's highest mountains! Imagine playing hide-and-seek there! When we say he "landed," it means his big airplane came down safely onto the ground. Just like when a bird comes down from the sky to its nest.

Who Did He Go To Talk To?

Mr. Vance went to Pakistan to meet with other important people. These people are called "officials" from another country named Iran. Iran is also a country in Asia, not too far from Pakistan. Think of it like this: when you want to solve a problem with someone, you meet them, right? You sit down and talk. That's what Mr. Vance did! He went to meet with the important people from Iran. Did you know? Iran is famous for its beautiful carpets and delicious food! Maybe Mr. Vance will get to try some nice food while he is there. These officials from Iran are like the helpers and leaders in their own country. They make important decisions for the people of Iran.

Why Are They Talking?

Now, this is the most important part! They are talking about a very big and serious problem. They want to make a special "deal" to end something called a "war." What is a war? A war is a very sad time when people from different countries or groups fight each other. They use loud machines and sometimes hurt people. Houses and schools can get broken. Children might not be able to go to school or play outside safely. War makes many people sad and scared. It's not a fun game; it's a real fight that causes lots of problems. No one likes war, especially not children or their families. Did you know? Talking is often called 'diplomacy'. It's a peaceful way for countries to solve problems, just like you talk to your friends instead of fighting. So, Mr. Vance and the people from Iran want to talk and find a way to stop this fighting. They want to bring an end to the war in Iran. This is a very brave and important thing to do.

What is a "Deal"?

When they talk about a "deal," it means they want to make a special promise or agreement. Imagine you and your friend make a promise to share toys and not argue. That's like a small deal! A big deal between countries means they will promise to stop fighting and help each other. It means they will agree on how to live peacefully together. This deal is like a big plan to bring peace. Peace means everyone is safe, happy, and can play and learn. It means no more fighting and no more sadness because of war. Did you know? When countries make a peace deal, it means children can go back to school, farmers can grow food, and families can live without fear. Isn't that wonderful?

Why is This Good News for Everyone?

When people stop fighting and make peace, it's good news for the whole world, even for us here in Africa! When there is peace in one part of the world, it helps make the whole world a safer and happier place. Imagine if all the children in the world could play together, learn together, and laugh together, no matter where they are from. That's what peace helps us do! We here in Africa, and children all over the globe, want to live in a world where everyone is kind to each other and problems are solved by talking, not fighting. Mr. Vance flying to Pakistan to talk about peace is a big step towards making that happen. He is trying to help create a future where everyone can live in peace. So, let's all hope that Mr. Vance and the officials from Iran have good talks and can make a wonderful deal for peace. Because peace is the best gift for all the children in the world, including you, my little African superstars! That's all for today from Kito the Reporter! Stay curious and keep smiling!
summary: US Vice President JD Vance flew to Pakistan to meet with officials from Iran and talk about making a special promise to end a sad war, hoping to bring peace to the people.
whyMatters: This is important because when leaders talk instead of fight, it helps stop wars. Stopping wars means children can be safe, go to school, and play without fear, making the world a happier place for everyone.

originalTitle: Lyse Doucet: Historic US-Iran talks must bridge deep distrust
title: Let's Talk! Big Grown-Ups Are Talking Peace!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa, here to share some exciting news from around the world! Today, we're talking about something super important: grown-ups talking to each other. You know how important it is to talk things out when you have a problem with a friend, right? Well, big countries do that too! It's how they make the world a better, friendlier place for all of us. Let's find out more about this big, important chat!

Two Big Countries: USA and Iran!

Today, our story is about two big countries: the United States of America, which we often call the USA, and a country called Iran. The USA is a very big country far away, across the ocean. It has lots of people and many famous places! Iran is also a big country, and it's in a part of the world called the Middle East. It has a very long history with ancient buildings and special traditions. Both countries are very important in the world. Did you know? The USA has a very famous statue called the Statue of Liberty! And Iran is famous for its beautiful carpets and delicious food like kebabs!

Why Are We Talking About Them?

Sometimes, even big countries have disagreements or don't understand each other very well. It's like when you and your friend might have a small misunderstanding. For a very long time, the USA and Iran haven't been talking to each other much. This can make things a little bit tricky. But now, something wonderful is happening! Important leaders from both countries are going to sit down face-to-face and have a big talk! This is super good news because talking is the first step to becoming friends and solving problems.

What Are "Talks"?

When we say "talks," we mean that important people, like leaders and special helpers from the USA and Iran, will meet together. They will sit in a room, maybe around a big table, and chat. They will listen to each other's ideas and try to find ways to agree. It's like when you and your teacher talk about how to make things better in class, or when you talk to your parents about what you want for dinner. These are not just casual chats; these are serious meetings to make big decisions that can help many, many people. Did you know? Sometimes, when countries talk, they have special people called "translators" who help them understand each other if they speak different languages!

A Long, Long Time Ago...

For a very, very long time – even before your parents were born! – the USA and Iran haven't had these kinds of face-to-face talks. They haven't been meeting with their most important leaders like they used to. This is why these new talks are so special! Think about it like two friends who stopped playing together for many years. Then, one day, they decide to meet up and try to be friends again. It takes courage and a big heart to do that. It shows they want to try and make things better for everyone. Did you know? The number of years they haven't talked this way is more than 40 years! That's a super long time!

Building Trust, Like Building Blocks!

When people don't talk for a long time, it's easy to start feeling "distrust." That means not really trusting each other. Maybe they worry about what the other country thinks or does. Imagine if you stopped talking to your friend for a long time. You might start to guess what they're thinking, and sometimes your guesses might be wrong! Talking helps you understand each other better. These big talks are like building a bridge of trust. Each time they talk, they add a new block to that bridge. It helps them to understand each other's feelings and ideas. It's a way to try and become friends again. Did you know? Trust is like a special invisible string that connects people. The stronger the string, the better friends you can be!

Why Is It Special Now?

These talks are not just any talks. They are the "highest-level engagement" since a very long time ago. What does that mean? It means the most important leaders and their most trusted helpers are meeting. It's like the head teachers from two different schools deciding to meet personally to solve a big school problem. This shows that both the USA and Iran really, really want to try and make things better. They are taking a very big step towards friendship and peace. This is a brave thing to do! Did you know? When leaders meet, they often shake hands as a sign of respect and to show they are ready to talk!

What Do We Hope For?

When these big grown-ups talk, we all hope for wonderful things! We hope they can find ways to agree and solve any problems they have. We hope they can build that bridge of trust and become better friends. When big countries are friendly, it helps the whole world feel safer and happier. It means fewer worries and more chances for everyone to learn and grow and play! We are all cheering for them to have very good talks.

Being Friends All Over the World!

So, there you have it, my amazing friends! This is a story about talking, about listening, and about trying to build friendship, even after a long time. It reminds us all that talking is super powerful. Whether it's you and your friend, or big countries like the USA and Iran, talking can always make things better. Let's all remember to talk things out, be kind, and try to understand each other. That’s how we make our world, and our Africa, a wonderful place for everyone! Keep shining!
summary: Important leaders from the USA and Iran are meeting for the first time in many years to talk and try to build trust and friendship.
whyMatters: These talks are important because when big countries like the USA and Iran talk, they can solve problems, become friends, and help make the whole world a safer and happier place for everyone.

originalTitle: Could Guardiola's best and Arteta's worst month decide title?
title: Who Will Win the Big Football Race?
content:
Hello, super sports fans! It's your kid-friendly reporter here, bringing you big news from the world of football! Imagine two super-fast cars racing to the finish line. They are neck and neck, and everyone is cheering! That's a bit like what's happening right now in England with two amazing football teams. These teams are called Arsenal and Manchester City. They are trying to win a very big, shiny cup called the Premier League trophy! Everyone loves watching them play, especially here in Africa where football is so popular!

What is the Premier League?

The Premier League is like the biggest football competition in England. Lots of teams play in it all year long. They play many, many games, and for every game, they get points. The team that gets the most points at the very end wins the big trophy! It's like a long, long treasure hunt, and the trophy is the treasure. Did you know? Many kids in Africa wake up early or stay up late just to watch these exciting Premier League games!

Meet Our Two Racing Teams!

First, we have **Arsenal**. They are a team from a big city called London. They wear red shirts, and they have been playing super well this year. For a long, long time, they were in the lead, like the fastest car in the race! Their fans were very, very happy. Then, we have **Manchester City**. They are another super strong team. They wear blue shirts, and they are very good at scoring goals. They are like a powerful engine, always moving forward. Both teams want to be the champion and lift that shiny trophy!

Why April Was So Important!

Imagine a race where one month, like April, is super-duper important. That's what happened in the Premier League! Teams played many games in April. Every game was like a big test. In football, teams get points for their games. If they **win**, they get 3 happy points! If they have a **draw** (that means they score the same number of goals, like 1-1 or 2-2), they get 1 point. If they **lose**, they get 0 points. These points add up, and the team with the most points wins the race!

What Happened to Arsenal in April?

Arsenal started April feeling very strong, like a lion! But then, in some games, they didn't win. They had a few draws, where they shared the points with the other team. Imagine you're running a race, and you slow down a little bit to tie your shoelaces. That's what happened to Arsenal. They got 1 point instead of 3 points in those games. This meant they didn't get as many points as they hoped. It was like dropping a few marbles when you're almost at the finish line. It made the race even closer!

What Happened to Manchester City in April?

But guess what? While Arsenal was having some tricky games, Manchester City was like a super-fast cheetah in April! They played many games, and they won almost all of them! Imagine catching up to someone in a race, and then zooming past them! This meant they got lots and lots of 3 points! They were getting closer and closer to Arsenal in the race for the trophy. Their players were super happy, and their fans were cheering very loudly! Did you know? Some of the best football players from all over the world, including Africa, play in the Premier League! They are super talented!

The Coaches and Their Teams

Every team has a special person called a coach, like a teacher or a captain for the players. For Arsenal, the coach is **Mikel Arteta**. He wants his team to play their very best football and win. For Manchester City, the coach is **Pep Guardiola**. He is very, very good at helping his team win many games. In April, it felt like Coach Pep's team had a super, super good month, and Coach Mikel's team had a bit of a tricky month. It's like one teacher's class got all A's, and another teacher's class got some B's and C's.

Why Does This Month Matter So Much?

This month, April, was like the decider! Because the football season is almost finished, there are only a few more games left to play. Every single point now is super, super important. It's like the very last sprint in a long race. The team that can get the most points in these final games will be the champion. It was a month that showed how quickly things can change in football! One moment you're leading, the next, someone is right beside you, or even ahead!

What Happens Next?

We have to wait and watch! The teams will play their very last games. Everyone is excited to see who will win the big, shiny Premier League trophy! Will it be Arsenal, who led for so long and tried their best? Or will it be Manchester City, who came zooming from behind in April? It's going to be a super exciting finish, like the last seconds of a tight game! Stay tuned, my sports friends, to find out who lifts the cup! Did you know? The Premier League trophy is made of silver and gold, and it is really heavy! It's a dream for every player to lift it! We love football!
summary: April was a very important month for two big football teams, Arsenal and Manchester City, in their race to win the shiny Premier League trophy.
whyMatters: This is important because how the teams played in April might decide who wins the big football cup at the end of the season!

originalTitle: De Zerbi 'not surprised' by reaction to Greenwood comments
title: De Zerbi 'not surprised' by reaction to Greenwood comments
content: Tottenham manager Roberto de Zerbi says he was "not surprised" by the reaction from supporters over comments he made about Mason Greenwood.
